qas.q:
The four-digit number appearing on a placard or an orange panel of a tank car is the:

A. UN product identification number
B. Capacity of the tank car
C. Last date the tank car was pressure tested
D. Tank car registration number

The NFPA 704 systems for identifying hazardous materials gives:

A. The general hazard and degree of severity on a fixed facility.
B. Specific product identification
C. The DOT hazard class
D. The UN hazard class
qas.a: The general hazard and degree of severity on a fixed facility.

qas.q:
A number 4 in the blue quadrant of the NFPA 704 system indicates that there is ___ risk on this category from the chemical involved.

A. A HIGH
B. A slight
C. No
D. A moderate

The fact that a product is water reactive would be indicated with _ in the NFPA 704 system:

A. ox with a line through it
B. Red
C. w with a line through it
D. Yellow
qas.a: W with a line through it

qas.q:
UN/DOT placards indicate general hazard recognition by:

A. Using numbers 0-4 to indicate relative risk
B. Always indicating the product name
C. Giving the UN hazard class number
D. The shape of the placard
qas.a: Giving the UN hazard class number

qas.q:
A bill of lading contains valuable information about the origin of the product and is the type of shipping paper used when transporting a product via:

A. Highway truck
B. Railway
C. Water
D. Air

A___ is placed on highway transport vehicles to identify the contents:

A. A placard
B. A Safety Data Sheet
C. A container ID
D. The manufacturers name

Where are pipeline owners required to place pipeline markings (and where can first responders expect to find them):

A. At the origin and destination points of the underground pipeline only
B. Wherever the underground pipeline passes in close proximity to highly populated areas
C. Only wherever the underground pipeline crosses state or provincial borders
D. At any place the underground pipeline crosses a mode of transportation
qas.a: D. At any place the underground pipeline crosses a mode of transportation

qas.q:
Of the following, which is the quickest available source of SDS for first responders at a hazardous materials incident?

A. The supplier of the material
B. The Local Emergency Planning Committee (LEPC)
C. CHEMTREC
D. OSHA

What does the red section stand for in the nfpa 704 placard?

A. Health hazard
B. Flammability
C. Special hazards
D. Reactivity

What does the yellow section stand for in the nfpa 704 placard?

A. Health hazard
B. Flammability
C. Special hazards
D. Instability

What does the blue section stand for in the nfpa 704 placard?

A. Health hazard
B. Flammability
C. Special hazards
D. Reactivity

What does the white section stand for in the nfpa 704 placard?

A. Health hazard
B. Flammability
C. Special hazards
D. Reactivity

Referring to the U.S. military marking system for hazardous materials, the symbol for a MASS EXPLOSION hazard is:

A. #3 in a triangle
B. #1 in a OCTOGON
C. #2 in a square
D. #4 in a circle

Referring to the U.S. military marking system for hazardous materials, the symbol for a explosion with fragment hazard is:

A. #3 in a triangle
B. #1 in a square
C. #2 in a X
D. #4 in a circle

Referring to the U.S. military marking system for hazardous materials, the symbol for a MASS FIRE hazard is:

A. #3 in a triangle
B. #1 in a square
C. #2 in a square
D. #4 in a circle

Referring to the U.S. military marking system for hazardous materials, the symbol for a MODERATE FIRE hazard is:

A. #3 in a triangle
B. #1 in a square
C. #2 in a square
D. #4 in a DIAMOND

U.S. military marking system for hazardous CHEMICAL, the symbol for HIGHLY TOXIC chemical agents, requires full protective clothing set 1


A. RED = DEAD
B. YELLOW=MELLOW
C. WHITE=BRIGHT

U.S. military marking system for hazardous CHEMICAL, the symbol for HARASSING AGENTS FULL, full protective clothing SET 2: (riot control agents and smoke)


A. RED = DEAD
B. YELLOW=MELLOW
C. WHITE=BRIGHT

U.S. military marking system for hazardous CHEMICAL , the symbol for spontaneously COMBUSTIBLE FULL protective clothing SET3:


A. RED = DEAD
B. YELLOW=MELLOW
C. WHITE=BRIGHT
PIPELINE MARKER MUST HAVE
qas.a:
NAME OF COMMODITY , COMPANY INFO AS WELL AS SIGNAL WORDS, CAUTION
WARNING
DANGER

qas.q: wear breathing apparatus
qas.a: indicates the presence of incendiary and readily flammable chemical agents that present an intense heat hazard.
